description_long: This is a rectangular, industrial-style outdoor air conditioning unit with a weathered, mottled white and gray metal casing. One side features a large, circular metal grille protecting the fan, with a smaller rectangular vent and a panel of horizontal slats nearby. The opposite side has a grid of smaller vents and a frosted access panel, likely for maintenance or controls. The unit stands on two sturdy black metal legs, with additional black piping and brackets attached for mounting or connection to building infrastructure. The surface is marked with visible stains, rust, and signs of outdoor exposure, giving it a rugged, utilitarian appearance. The overall design suggests it is intended for fixed installation in an urban or commercial environment.
description: A weathered, rectangular outdoor air conditioner with a large circular fan grille, multiple vents, and black metal legs and piping, showing signs of rust and outdoor use.
description_view_0: This view shows the end with a frosted access panel and a grid of small rectangular vents below it, as well as part of the black mounting bracket.
description_view_1: The back side is mostly plain, with a mottled white and gray surface, visible stains, and the two black metal legs supporting the unit.
description_view_2: This end features a frosted panel and a single opening for piping, with the black mounting bracket and pipes extending outward.
description_view_3: The main side displays a large circular metal grille for the fan, a smaller rectangular vent, and a panel of horizontal slats, all set in a weathered metal casing.
attribute_grille_pattern: The main grille is circular with concentric rings and radial spokes, forming a fan-protective mesh; a secondary grille features horizontal slats.
attribute_exhaust_vent_design: Single large circular exhaust vent with a concentric grille on the main side, oriented outward; additional smaller rectangular vents present.
